items.title: May 31, 1990: The Portland Trail Blazers advance to their second NBA Finals in franchise history with a 112-109 win over the Phoenix Suns in Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 31, 1990: The Portland Trail Blazers advance to their second NBA Finals in franchise history with a 112-109 win over the Phoenix Suns in Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals.

Clyde Drexler had 23 PTS/10 REB/7 AST and Terry Porter had 23 PTS/7 AST to lead the Blazers.

items.title: The Thunder absolutely need to trade Chet this summer because it's impossible for a 24-year-old All-NBA player to bounce back from a bad playoff series. Nobody's ever done it before. He's doomed forever now.

items.body_excerpt: The Thunder absolutely need to trade Chet this summer because it's impossible for a 24-year-old All-NBA player to bounce back from a bad playoff series. Nobody's ever done it before. He's doomed forever now.

items.title: OKC Thunder efficiency at the rim in the playoffs:
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
OKC Thunder efficiency at the rim in the playoffs:

First two rounds (8 games):
130/171 | 76.0%

Western Conference Finals (7 games): 82/150 | 54.7%

At 76% efficiency (114/150), the Thunder would have scored 64 more points at the rim in the Spurs series.

Data: PivotFade.com
